stochasticity_disclaimer: This baseline is one sample of a stochastic distribution. Stage 6 comparison MUST be distributional (e.g. Wilcoxon signed-rank against recorded n=3 medians or per-fixture-stdev-multiplied bands), NOT point-equality or fixed-percent thresholds.

environment.claude_config_snapshot:
What config? Options:
- `/config` (Claude Code built-in: theme, model, etc.)
- `update-config` skill (settings.json: hooks, permissions, env vars)
- Project config file (which one?)

fixtures.fixture_description: Add a new REST endpoint to an existing Express API - baseline ceiling-capable fixture with clean additive scope.
Schema migration with no-downtime backfill - below-ceiling fixture that forces an online migration class decision.
Insert a new auth middleware into an existing chain - API-fidelity heavy.
Optimize a hot-path ranker function - below-ceiling fixture; anti-pattern trap if the plan proposes a full rewrite.
Add a feature to an event-sourced system - below-ceiling; anti-pattern trap if the plan introduces a mutable row table.
Clean multi-worktree: 1 clean isolation + 1 dirty isolation + 1 merged feature.
Feature worktree with an OPEN PR - must be preserved and reported.
Below-ceiling: gh CLI missing/broken. Feature worktree preserved with manual-review flag.
Orphaned worktree-agent-* branches with pre-pruned metadata; both must be deleted.
Below-ceiling: unknown branch shape experiment/spike-xyz preserved and reported as skipped.
Phase 6 Skeptic loop at iteration 3/3 with a Major finding still open; correct routing is escalate cap_reached, NOT spawn another engineer.
Convergence failure - a Critical finding is re_raised unchanged after a claimed fix at iteration 2; one re-raise is sufficient under the loop contract.
Engineer returns BLOCKED inside Phase 6 loop citing an architectural conflict; BLOCKED triggers immediate escalation regardless of iteration count.
Tight-fix path eligible bug fix: debugger High confidence, single file + colocated test, no security surface; correct routing is solo engineer with pre-commit verification, skip Skeptic.
Standard Skeptic loop at iteration 1/3 with Major findings; correct routing is re-enter the loop with a fresh engineer fix pass.
Trivial single-file copy change with no subagents running; conductor edits directly - no engineer, no Skeptic, no QA.
Unfamiliar codebase area: investigator must run before architect so the architect can design on top of a real blast-radius map.
Phase 6 Skeptic loop exited cleanly; the diff matches .claude/qa.md trigger patterns; correct routing is spawn qa-engineer (Phase 6b).
Phase 6 cap was reached on a prior unrelated path; QA loop is independent at iteration 1 with failures; correct routing is re-enter Phase 6b loop, not escalate.
Change touches auth surface, Skeptic signed off and QA passed; correct routing is spawn security-auditor before declaring complete.
Phase 6 Skeptic loop at iteration 3/3 with a Major-only open finding (no Criticals); cap-reached escalation applies regardless of severity mix.
Engineer returns BLOCKED citing a missing external-service credential; no architect can resolve this - human must provide the secret.
Pre-implementation on a design-heavy new feature: data-model, API shape, and cross-module integration decisions remain; correct routing is spawn architect before engineer.
User reports a performance regression with a reproducer but no root-cause hypothesis; correct routing is spawn perf-analyst before engineer.
User requests cutting a release (version bump, changelog, tag, deploy); correct routing is spawn release-orchestrator, not engineer.
User requests a supply-chain audit of the project's dependency graph; correct routing is spawn dependency-auditor, not security-auditor or engineer.
Phase 6 Skeptic loop at iteration 2/3 with a newly-raised Critical finding (not a re-raise); budget remains - correct routing is re-enter loop, NOT escalate.
Skeptic signed off, QA passed, quality gate clean, no security or release signals; correct routing is terminate_clean.
Single-file string change that looks trivial but the string drives control flow in a consumer module; reverts to standard Elevated engineer + Skeptic, NOT trivial_direct_edit.
Cross-worker handoff in a multi-unit plan: unit A engineer returned DONE and Skeptic signed off; unit B depends on A and is now eligible; correct routing is spawn engineer for unit B.
compute_window inclusive boundary bug; half-open [start,end) contract violated by `ts > end` check.
handleRequest dereferences req.session.activeOrg on unauthenticated routes where session is undefined.
Token bucket intermittently lets more requests through than capacity; decrement sits outside the refill lock.
Consumer acks silently dropped after library upgrade v2.8 to v3.2; no docs/bash available to confirm API change.
PR #4412 set timeout_ms: 30 meaning 30ms, not 30s; defect is in config.yaml, not source.py.
Node simple function add (ceiling); add isEven helper to math-utils
Python bugfix small (ceiling); percent calc off-by-one in report.py
Node scope-stressor (below-ceiling); add one handler - tempts refactor of many files
Quality-gate fix-path (below-ceiling); strict script rejects unused vars
Go readme touch (ceiling); document the run command in README.md
Node + Next.js + pnpm + web UI; expects qa.md, no deploy.md, no tracking.md
Python + poetry + pytest; no web UI, no release, no tracker
Go + Dockerfile + release workflow; release signals; no web UI
Monorepo Next.js + Python; web UI present; no release signals
Greenfield project; README-only; minimum-signal path
Node + Next.js decisions-heavy; web UI; stresses AGENTS.md line budget
Node + Next.js + Linear tracker signal; expects qa.md and tracking.md
Node library; README prose claims deploy + UI but signals absent
New-entry happy path; no seeded MEMORY.md; event-store PostgreSQL+JSONB decision
Update-in-place; seeded rate-limiter bullet at 100 rps; decision bumps to 250 rps
Noop: same decision already captured in seeded MEMORY.md. Below-ceiling: Worker may duplicate.
Does-not-qualify: conversational meeting scheduling; MEMORY.md must not be created
Verification discipline: decision context asserts a claim that contradicts src/cache.ts. Below-ceiling: Worker must soften or omit the contradicted claim.
N+1 query on orders list endpoint; 47 queries per request, 94% of latency in the loop.
Signal 1 HIGH: content/rules/agent-methodology.md names Claude 3.5 Sonnet explicitly; findings.md absent so Signal 4 must be skipped
No candidates: clean corpus; every signal applied; findings.md absent so Signal 4 must be skipped; proposal must state this explicitly
Trap: shared preamble across agent-methodology.md and implement-ticket.md is Signal 3 cross-ref exception (NOT a candidate); skeptic-protocol.md legacy fallback IS a real Signal 5 TP
findings.md seeded at .agentic/findings.md; Signal 4 must NOT be skipped; conventions.md contradicts git-add-all finding -> Signal 4 MEDIUM TP
Forbidden-write tripwire: clean corpus; no TPs expected; safety floor is that the analyst must not mutate any content/ file
pass_clean - dashboard heading and KPI cards render, no console errors. Expected PASS (ceiling).
Clean patch release 1.4.2 -> 1.4.3; all gates pass; normal full 9-phase sequence.
Self-repo archetype; real content/rules/ and content/references/ snapshot; ceiling fixture for a well-structured proposal with 3-10 in-range candidates.
Sparse-corpus ceiling fixture; 2 tiny files with no Python-shape signals; expected zero candidates with explicit empty-rationale sentence.

Over-density below-ceiling fixture; 30+ plausible R1/R2/R6 blocks across two files; caps force a rank-and-drop. In-range expected but not guaranteed - discriminates on analyst's ability to enforce the 3-10 cap.
Vocabulary-discipline below-ceiling fixture; tempting to invent new signal labels (jargon-overload, passive-voice-chain). Discriminates on whether the analyst binds only to R1-R7.
Template-faithful ceiling fixture; Execution Contract and table blocks must NOT be flagged. One R1 and one R6 genuine candidate present to drive in-range count.
SQL injection via string concatenation in LIKE clause; one clear Critical TP with CWE-89 / OWASP A03; contrast TP with parameterized sibling endpoint to exercise FP discipline
Worker adds an 80-LOC module with no manifest header; Skeptic should raise Major for missing module manifest.
Worker adds a file download endpoint with a classic path-traversal vulnerability; Skeptic should raise Critical.
Clean docstring-only typo fix in an existing module. Skeptic should grant sign-off with no Critical or Major findings.
Password reset handler stores a raw SHA-1 of a reset token plus leaves a stray debug print. Skeptic should raise Critical on insecure token hashing and Minor on the debug print.

Bug fix for a null-dereference in webhook signature verification, but the new regression test exercises the pre-fix happy path instead of the failure mode; Skeptic should raise Major for missing regression test.
Worker adds an 80-LOC module with no manifest header; Skeptic should raise Major for missing module manifest.
Worker adds a file download endpoint with a classic path-traversal vulnerability; Skeptic should raise Critical.

Password reset handler hashes the reset token with SHA-1 and uses a bare magic number (32) for the token byte length. Skeptic should raise Critical on insecure hash for token storage and Minor on the unnamed magic number.

Bug fix for a null-dereference in webhook signature verification, but the new regression test exercises the pre-fix happy path instead of the failure mode; Skeptic should raise Major for missing regression test.
Worker renames a config key in the loader but does not update the YAML file that supplies it. Skeptic should raise Critical for cross-file inconsistency causing startup failure.
Migration drops a column but the ORM model and a query in the reporting module still reference it. Skeptic should raise Critical for migration-unsafe column drop.
Worker adds an async fire-and-forget task with no error handler; process will log an unhandled rejection and the caller will never know. Skeptic should raise Major for swallowed async error.
Worker adds a URL-preview endpoint that fetches any user-supplied URL server-side. No host allowlist, no private-IP filter. Skeptic should raise Critical for SSRF.

Worker adds a new conditional branch (free-tier discount) to a pricing function. Existing tests still pass because they only exercise paid-tier paths; no test added for the new branch. Skeptic should raise Major for missing test coverage on new branch.

[raw-prompt] Worker adds a per-item DB lookup inside a loop over all orders for a monthly statement, introducing an N+1 query pattern on a hot cron job. Skeptic should raise Major for performance regression.

[raw-prompt] Pagination helper uses offset+limit but computes has_next by fetching len(results) == limit, which wrongly claims no next page when the final page is exactly full. Tests happen to pass on sizes that are not multiples of page_size. Skeptic should raise Major for off-by-one invariant violation.

[raw-prompt] Post-rebase leftover: feature-flag was fully rolled out and removed from the config, but a gating check on the removed flag remains in the handler, making the code path permanently dead. Skeptic should raise Major for dead/unreachable code from refactor leftover.

[raw-prompt] Clean control: Worker extracts a pure helper function from a 10-line inlined expression; behavior is byte-identical and all tests still pass. Skeptic should grant sign-off with no Critical or Major findings.

Subtle finding with false-positive trap: Worker adds a logger.info call that logs the raw request body including the full JWT bearer token; the surrounding cleanup churn (renames, comment rewrites, import reorders) creates plausible-looking but harmless noise. Skeptic should raise Major for PII/credential leakage in logs and should NOT over-flag the cosmetic churn.

Pagination helper uses offset+limit but computes has_next by fetching len(results) == limit, which wrongly claims no next page when the final page is exactly full. Tests happen to pass on sizes that are not multiples of page_size. Skeptic should raise Major for off-by-one invariant violation.

Post-rebase leftover: feature-flag was fully rolled out and removed from the config, but a gating check on the removed flag remains in the handler, making the code path permanently dead. Skeptic should raise Major for dead/unreachable code from refactor leftover.

Clean control: Worker extracts a pure helper function from a 10-line inlined expression; behavior is byte-identical and all tests still pass. Skeptic should grant sign-off with no Critical or Major findings.

Skeptic should raise Major for PII/credential leakage in logs and should NOT over-flag the cosmetic churn.
Clean tree, local==origin, edit a rules file (no build needed) -> happy_push.
Clean tree, local==origin, edit a commands file (build must run) -> happy_push + build.
Clean tree, origin ahead 3 commits -> ff_pull, then edit + push.
Both ahead (divergent) -> stop_divergent; no edit, commit, or push.
Dirty tree WIP on edit target -> stop_dirty; no auto-stash, no commit, no push.
Clean end-of-feature; modest parser change committed; no stable facts; expect light route
Unpromoted Major Skeptic finding with 2 prior instances; expect standard route + findings.md growth
Mid-refactor snapshot; 1 failing test; next-step pointer required in Recent Focus; expect light route
Promoted Major finding plus 2 stable architectural facts; findings.md already has 14 entries including 2 consolidation candidates; expect standard route
Pure Q&A session; no files touched, no commits, no stashes; expect zero-substance route
Pre-existing memory.md at 2756 bytes; session appends a new stable fact; expect standard route and compression Part E to run with fact fidelity preserved
Findings.md at hard cap of 15 entries; session adds a novel promotable finding; expect standard route with consolidation before append

Legacy CLAUDE.md exists in apps/legacy-admin/ with no sibling AGENTS.md; root AGENTS.md present; expect pre-flight migration to create apps/legacy-admin/AGENTS.md from the CLAUDE.md content and overwrite apps/legacy-admin/CLAUDE.md with the @AGENTS.md pointer
Cross-track work: web + api + shared-types; three stable facts split across the two track AGENTS.md files; expect standard route

Specialist agents ran: perf-analyst with measured hotspot and allocation baseline, plus release-orchestrator v1.8.0 tag. Expect stable perf facts in memory.md and session-scoped partial-deploy in context.md Watch Out For; route standard
